questions.question:
Match the scientists given in column I with their work given in column II and select the correct answer using the codes given below.

 
 
 Column I 
 Column II
 
 
 (A) Priestley
 (i) Determined the action spectrum of chlorophyll
 
 
 (B) Engelmann
 (ii) Demonstrated that given amount of light reduced more carbon dioxide when allowed to fall on the cells intermittently
 
 
 (C) Niel
 (iii) Demonstrated that Photosynthesis is essentially a light-dependent reaction
 
 
 (D) Warburg
 (iv) Revealed the essential role of air in the growth of green plants
questions.question_html:
Match the scientists given in column I with their work given in column II and select the correct answer using the codes given below.

Column I Column II
(A) Priestley (i) Determined the action spectrum of chlorophyll
(B) Engelmann (ii) Demonstrated that given amount of light reduced more carbon dioxide when allowed to fall on the cells intermittently
(C) Niel (iii) Demonstrated that Photosynthesis is essentially a light-dependent reaction
(D) Warburg (iv) Revealed the essential role of air in the growth of green plants

questions.question:
Select the statement that gives correct outline of the main events in photosynthesis.

 In the chloroplast, oxygen reacts with glucose to produce water and carbon dioxide in the presence of light.
 In the presence of light, carbon dioxide combines to an acceptor compound which is then reduced by hydrogen obtained from water
 Water splits in the presence of light and the resulting hydroxyl group combines with a compound which has incorporated carbon dioxide
 Carbon dioxide combines with an acceptor compound which is then converted into sugar using assimilatory power produced during biochemical phase of photosynthesis.
questions.question_html:

  1. In the chloroplast, oxygen reacts with glucose to produce water and carbon dioxide in the presence of light.
  2. In the presence of light, carbon dioxide combines to an acceptor compound which is then reduced by hydrogen obtained from water
  3. Water splits in the presence of light and the resulting hydroxyl group combines with a compound which has incorporated carbon dioxide
  4. Carbon dioxide combines with an acceptor compound which is then converted into sugar using assimilatory power produced during biochemical phase of photosynthesis.

questions.question:
How many statements are correct for effect of Carbon dioxide concentration on photosynthesis?

 Increase in concentration up to 0.05 percent can cause an increase in CO_(2) fixation rates; beyond this the levels can become damaging over longer periods.
 The C_(3) and C_(4) plants respond differently to CO_(2) concentrations.
 At low light conditions neither C_(3) nor C_(4) responds to high CO_(2) conditions.
 At high light intensities, both C_(3) and C_(4) plants show increase in the rates of photosynthesis.
 C_(4) plants show saturation at about 360 $\mu \mathrm{lL}^{-1}$ while C_(3) responds to increased CO_(2) concentration and saturation is seen only beyond 450 $\mu \mathrm{lL}^{-1}$ .
 Current availability of CO_(2) levels in atmosphere is limiting to the C_(3) plants.
questions.question_html:
How many statements are correct for effect of Carbon dioxide concentration on photosynthesis?

  1. Increase in concentration up to 0.05 percent can cause an increase in CO2 fixation rates; beyond this the levels can become damaging over longer periods.
  2. The C3 and C4 plants respond differently to CO2 concentrations.
  3. At low light conditions neither C3 nor C4 responds to high CO2 conditions.
  4. At high light intensities, both C3 and C4 plants show increase in the rates of photosynthesis.
  5. C4 plants show saturation at about 360 {tex}\mu \mathrm{lL}^{-1}{/tex} while C3 responds to increased CO2 concentration and saturation is seen only beyond 450 {tex}\mu \mathrm{lL}^{-1}{/tex}.
  6. Current availability of CO2 levels in atmosphere is limiting to the C3 plants.
